format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is the '# <title>' H1; no leading YAML frontmatter. Sidecar reading.meta.json present and parses with word_count, estimated_read_minutes, citations_used, sections. Exactly the six required ## sections in order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References.

length.notes: SOFT FINDING: 1520 words is ~16% under the deep floor (1800). Per the length ruling, depth=deep was a human HITL#2 light-week budget call and the builder honestly elaborated the worked example without padding (no-pad principle). Not hard-failed; recorded so it surfaces at HITL#3/#4. read-aloud minutes 1520/230 = 6.6, sidecar lists 8.

citations.notes: Markers [1][2][3] all used inline and all present in the References bibliography; no orphan markers, no orphan bibliography entries; no fabricated markers. citations_used [1,2,3] matches.

forward_references.notes: 8.6 calibration is name-and-deferred only ('Calibration is a different question, covered in 8.6.', line 100) — not taught. Reuse of 8.3/8.4 (TP/FP/FN, precision/recall, F1 harmonic mean) is explicitly allowed and flagged '(from 8.3)'/'(from 8.4)'. No undefined-forward dependencies.

faithfulness.notes: Every sampled assertion traces to topic_corpus.md (four outcomes, naming mnemonic, 2x2 matrix, accuracy formula + rare-class caution, precision/recall/F1 cell formulas, worked example, six-step procedure, best-practice Do/Don'ts). No new claims. CRUCIAL number check: only the two corpus matrices appear — spam TP6/FP3/FN2/TN9 (total 20) and fraud TP8/FP2/FN4/TN11 (total 25). No numbers beyond the corpus.

arithmetic.notes: Spam worked example verified: total 6+2+3+9=20 OK; accuracy (6+9)/20=15/20=0.75 OK; precision 6/(6+3)=6/9=0.667 -> 0.67 OK; recall 6/(6+2)=6/8=0.75 OK; F1 2*(0.67*0.75)/(0.67+0.75)=1.005/1.42=0.708 -> shown 0.71 OK (intermediate 2*0.50/1.42 is a display rounding of 0.5025; final value correct). Fraud matrix is an unsolved exercise — no computed values asserted, nothing to verify, counts match corpus.

beginner_readability.notes: Concrete-first (spam-filter scenario opens Overview before any abstraction). Acronyms TP/FP/FN/TN spelled out and defined at first use; 'binary' glossed as 'yes/no'; 'harmonic mean' carried from 8.4. No jargon dump. Plain register, minimal hedging, short sentences.

formatting.notes: Confusion matrices rendered as tables; procedure as numbered steps; best practices and cell-formulas as bullet lists; bold-term/plain-meaning definition shape used. Paragraphs <=4 sentences. Inline examples appear in Key Concepts (rare-class 99% example), not only in Worked Example.

scope.notes: On the headline. Accuracy is newly taught (in-scope for this topic). No multi-class, ROC, AUC, macro/micro/weighted averaging, or PR-curve content. Calibration named-and-deferred only. No scope_creep.

assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights in learner prose.

diagram_embed.notes: Diagram embedded inline at line 26 (![A 2x2 confusion matrix grid](./diagram.png)) with caption. embeds_diagram=true in sidecar. PNG exists per orchestrator confirmation (topic path exceeds Windows MAX_PATH; absence not raised).

verdict: PASS. Reading is faithful to corpus, on-headline (accuracy newly taught; calibration deferred to 8.6), beginner-clear, well-formatted, correctly cited, diagram embedded, and all worked arithmetic is correct (spam: acc 0.75 / prec 0.67 / rec 0.75 / F1 0.71). One SOFT finding: word count 1520 is ~16% under the deep floor (1800-2200) — accepted per no-pad ruling and recorded for HITL#3/#4 visibility. No hard defects.
